# Harness, Not Committee

## Core position

The system should not wait on committees to produce routine marketing work.

But the system still needs a harness:

- clear inputs,
- persistent Markdown memory,
- known product facts,
- platform-specific packet structure,
- result logs,
- repeatable experiment naming,
- API payload descriptions,
- campaign run history.

This is not bureaucracy. It is context engineering.

## What the harness should do

| Harness element | Why it exists |
|---|---|
| Product facts | Prevents hallucinated offers/features |
| Buyer language memory | Keeps copy grounded in real phrases |
| Tactic cards | Lets the agent select strategies intentionally |
| Source notes | Prevents folklore from replacing research |
| Platform packet templates | Makes API execution repeatable |
| Run logs | Creates campaign memory |
| Learning memos | Turns results into future intelligence |
| Permission profile | Lets the agent run without random interruption |

## What the harness should not do

- It should not force a human to approve every sentence.
- It should not turn the agent into a keyword router.
- It should not freeze every campaign into a fixed SOP.
- It should not prevent exploration.

## Practical rule

If the agent can write a Markdown artifact that makes the decision inspectable later, let it run and record the action.
